mnemonics: Backward Induction: Solve Like a Movie Plot Twist (Start from the End)
keyPoints: Start from the final subgame (last decision point) and determine the optimal action for the player there.
Move backward to the preceding subgame, knowing what will happen in the future, and solve for the optimal action there.
Continue this process backward to the very first decision. The resulting strategy profile is a subgame perfect equilibrium.
commonMistakes: Solving the game forward (from the beginning) instead of backward (from the end), which can lead to non-credible threats being included in the equilibrium.
Failing to consider all possible actions at a node when working backward, which can cause an incomplete or incorrect analysis of the subgame.
